summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a high‑energy ’67 show, highlighted by a smoking The Other One and soaring Lovelight.
review: The recording delivers surprisingly clear, well‑balanced audio for its era, with every instrument audible despite only one drummer on the kit. Musically the band is tight and energetic – Jerry’s guitar roars through The Other One, Lovelight bursts with fire, and the early psychedelic set flows smoothly from Morning Dew to New Potato Caboose.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 5) and enthusiastic comments about both performance and sound quality; strong show with multiple standout songs and a clear, well‑mixed soundboard source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sound is crisp and all instruments are audible; reviewers note good fidelity for a 1967 tape. Only one drummer is present, which some mention as a historic quirk.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy, tight playing with seamless transitions; the band moves from blues‑rock numbers into psychedelic jams (Cryptical/Other One) without sloppiness.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is described as brutal, vicious, sweet‑passionate and even “magma discharge,” delivering one of the greatest early solos on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work gets praised for counterpoint with Jerry and strong ensemble playing during the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s rhythm guitar and vocals are noted as solid, especially on Beat It.
band_member_comments.Drums: Only one drummer (Bill) is present; reviewers note the tight drumming despite Mickey not yet joining.
